master_row.title: Vacuum Fluctuation Operator (TZP)
master_row.canonical_statement: This operator quantifies the magnitude of vacuum fluctuations of a given mode in the presence of the TZP.
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as an algebraic definition relating ħ, ω, η.
master_row.dictionary: The vacuum fluctuation operator (V_T) acts on a field mode (_) as (V_T _ = 2,_,(2_0)), where (_0) is determined by the spectral density index.
master_row.encyclopedia: The vacuum fluctuation operator assigns each mode a zero-point amplitude corrected by the TZP spectral scale. Interpretive role: This operator is the local quantum-noise input for later vacuum-energy, decoherence, and thermodynamic statements. It gives the canon a direct modewise handle on how the anchor modifies nominally empty states.
